body:
## Summary
- Adds a self-contained authentication service (Node + TypeScript + Express): `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`, and a protected `GET /auth/me`.
- Exposes the reusable `requireAuth` middleware and `verifyToken` util that the notes CRUD (#2) and sync (#3) services will build on; storage sits behind a `UserRepository` interface (in-memory for now) so a real DB drops in without touching auth logic.
- Security per the reviewed plan: bcrypt-hashed passwords (cost 12), stateless JWTs with a 1h expiry, `AUTH_JWT_SECRET` required outside dev, generic `invalid credentials` on login to prevent user enumeration, case-insensitive duplicate-email rejection, and user responses that never leak the password hash.

Closes #1.

## Test plan
- `npm test` — 12 Jest + supertest cases, all passing: register (success/duplicate/validation/hash-at-rest), login (success/wrong-password/unknown-email-generic), token round-trip + tamper rejection, and `requireAuth` (no token / bad token / valid token).
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean typecheck.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(auth): add user authentication service
